name: Relativity (Special & General)
description: Einstein's theories revolutionizing understanding of space, time, gravity, and the universe, essential for technologies like GPS.

name: Quantum Physics
description: Fundamental theory in physics describing nature at the smallest scales of energy levels of atoms and subatomic particles.

name: Isotope Separation
description: The process of concentrating specific isotopes of a chemical element by separating them from other isotopes, critical for nuclear applications.

name: Nuclear Fission
description: Splitting of atomic nuclei, releasing vast amounts of energy.

name: Particle Accelerators
description: Devices that accelerate subatomic particles to high velocities, used to study fundamental physics.

name: Plasma Physics
description: Study of the fourth state of matter (plasma), crucial for fusion research and astrophysics.

name: Nuclear Fusion Research
description: Theoretical and experimental efforts to harness nuclear fusion energy, primarily through tokamaks and stellarators.

name: Catalysis
description: The study and application of catalysts to increase the rate of chemical reactions, fundamental to industrial chemistry.

name: Polymer Chemistry
description: A sub-discipline of chemistry that focuses on the chemical synthesis, structure, and chemical/physical properties of polymers and macromolecules.

sources.title: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 1963 - Speed Read

name: Advanced Synthetic Materials
description: Development of high-performance synthetic materials like nylon, teflon, and kevlar through polymer chemistry.

name: Composite Materials (Carbon Fiber)
description: Materials made from two or more constituent materials with significantly different properties, such as carbon fiber-reinforced polymers, offering high strength-to-weight ratios.

name: Nanotechnology (Early Research)
description: The manipulation of matter on an atomic and molecular scale, laying the groundwork for future nanoscale engineering.

name: Material Science (Lightweight Alloys)
description: Development of strong, lightweight metal alloys, particularly aluminum and titanium based, for aerospace and other applications.

name: Furnace Technology (High Temperature)
description: Development of furnaces capable of achieving very high temperatures for material processing.

name: Filters & Membranes (Early)
description: Development of materials and techniques for selective separation of substances, e.g., for water purification or air filtering.

name: Clean Rooms
description: Controlled environments with low levels of pollutants, essential for manufacturing sensitive equipment like semiconductors and for scientific research.

name: Crystal Growth Techniques
description: Methods for producing high-purity single crystals, essential for semiconductors and other advanced materials.

name: Precision Mechanics & Miniaturization
description: Engineering of highly precise and small-scale mechanical components and systems.

name: Numerical Control (Automated Machining)
description: Automation of machine tools using programmed commands on punched tape, a precursor to CNC.

name: Electronics
description: Science of controlling electrical energy in which electrons have a fundamental role, including vacuum tubes and semiconductors.

name: Tungsten Filament Production
description: Manufacturing high-melting-point tungsten wires for light bulbs and vacuum tubes.

name: Vacuum Tubes (Electron Control)
description: Electronic components controlling electric current in a vacuum, foundational for early electronics like radio and computers.

name: Cathode Ray Tube (CRT) Displays
description: Vacuum tubes generating images by directing an electron beam onto a phosphorescent screen.

name: Semiconductors
description: Materials with electrical conductivity between a conductor and an insulator, basis of modern electronics.

name: Transistors
description: Semiconductor devices used to amplify or switch electronic signals and electrical power, replacing vacuum tubes.

name: Photolithography
description: Process using light to transfer a geometric pattern from a photomask to a light-sensitive chemical on a substrate, key for IC manufacturing.

name: Integrated Circuits (Microchips)
description: Miniaturized electronic circuits (chips) that drastically reduce size and cost of electronics.

sources.title: 1959: Practical Monolithic Integrated Circuit Concept Patented
1959: Invention of the Planar Manufacturing Process

name: LED Lighting (Efficient Illumination)
description: Light-emitting diodes used for general lighting, offering high efficiency and long lifespan.

sources.title: The Nobel Prize in Physics 2014: Popular Information

name: Liquid Crystal Displays (LCD Screens)
description: Flat-panel displays using the light-modulating properties of liquid crystals.

name: Algorithms & Computation Theory
description: Formal study of algorithms, computability, and complexity, forming the theoretical basis of computer science.

name: Magnetic Tape Data Storage
description: Storing digital data on magnetic tape, an early form of mass computer storage.

name: Computers (Mainframe/Early)
description: Electronic devices for storing and processing data using vacuum tubes or transistors.

name: Microprocessors (CPU on a Chip)
description: Central processing units fabricated on a single integrated circuit, enabling the personal computer revolution.

sources.title: Announcing a New Era of Integrated Electronics

name: Software Engineering
description: Systematic application of engineering principles to the design, development, and maintenance of software.

name: Operating Systems (OS Computer Management)
description: Software that manages computer hardware and software resources, providing common services for computer programs.

name: Personal Computers
description: Affordable computers for individual use, powered by microprocessors.

name: Object-Oriented Programming
description: A programming paradigm based on the concept of 'objects', which can contain data and code: a major evolution in software design.

name: Graphical User Interface (GUI)
description: Visual way of interacting with computers using windows, icons, and menus.

name: 3D Computer Graphics
description: Generating and displaying three-dimensional images in a two-dimensional space (like a screen), crucial for modern gaming, simulation, and design.

name: Computer-Aided Design (CAD Software)
description: Use of computer systems to aid in the creation, modification, analysis, or optimization of a design.

name: Computer-Aided Manufacturing (CAM Systems)
description: Use of software to control machine tools and related machinery in the manufacturing of workpieces.

name: 3D Printing (Additive Manufacturing)
description: Constructing three-dimensional objects from a CAD model or digital 3D model, layer by layer.

name: Telecommunications
description: Transmission of information over significant distances by electronic means (radio, television, telephone networks).

name: Information Theory
description: Mathematical study of the quantification, storage, and communication of digital information.

name: Data Compression
description: The process of encoding information using fewer bits than the original representation, crucial for storage and transmission.

name: Encryption (Modern Cryptography, Public Key)
description: Development of advanced cryptographic systems, including public-key cryptography, for secure communication.

name: Computer Networking
description: The practice of linking two or more computing devices together for the purpose of sharing data, forming local and wide area networks.

name: Internet Protocols (TCP/IP, ARPANET)
description: Development of foundational communication protocols enabling computer networking, originating with ARPANET.

name: Masers (Early Development)
description: Devices that produce coherent electromagnetic waves through amplification by stimulated emission, precursor to lasers (microwave range).

name: Lasers
description: Devices that emit light through a process of optical amplification based on the stimulated emission of electromagnetic radiation.

name: Fiber Optics
description: Technology that uses glass (or plastic) threads (fibers) to transmit data as light pulses.

name: Internet
description: A global system of interconnected computer networks using the Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP).

name: Hypertext Systems
description: Text displayed on a computer with references (hyperlinks) to other text that the reader can immediately access.

name: World Wide Web
description: An information system where documents and other web resources are identified by URLs, interlinked by hypertext links, and accessible via the Internet.

name: Databases (Relational DBMS)
description: Systems for organizing, storing, and retrieving large amounts of structured data using relational models.

name: Data Centers (Early)
description: Facilities housing computer systems and associated components, such as telecommunications and storage systems.

name: Virtualization (Early VM Tech)
description: Creation of virtual versions of computer hardware platforms, operating systems, or storage devices on a single physical machine.

name: Cloud Computing & Distributed Systems
description: Delivery of computing services—including servers, storage, databases, networking, software—over the Internet.

name: Search Engines
description: Software systems designed to carry out web searches, which means to search the World Wide Web in a systematic way for particular information.

name: E-commerce
description: The buying and selling of goods or services using the internet, and the transfer of money and data to execute these transactions.

name: Social Media
description: Interactive technologies that facilitate the creation and sharing of information, ideas, and other forms of expression via virtual communities and networks.

name: Blockchain & Cryptocurrency
description: A decentralized, distributed, and oftentimes public, digital ledger consisting of records called blocks that is used to record transactions across many computers.

name: Cybernetics
description: The study of control and communication in animals and machines, influencing AI and robotics.

name: Artificial Intelligence (Early)
description: Machine systems capable of tasks requiring basic human-like intelligence (e.g., expert systems, symbolic reasoning, search algorithms).

sources.title: A Proposal for the Dartmouth Summer Research Project on Artificial Intelligence

name: Machine Learning (Early Algorithms)
description: Development of algorithms that allow computer systems to learn from data without being explicitly programmed (e.g. decision trees, support vector machines).

name: Big Data Analytics & Computational Statistics
description: Analyzing extremely large data sets to uncover patterns, correlations, and insights using advanced computational methods.

name: Biochemistry
description: Study of chemical processes within and relating to living organisms.

name: Antibiotics
description: Drugs that kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, revolutionizing medicine and public health.

name: X-ray Crystallography
description: Technique for determining the atomic and molecular structure of a crystal by analyzing diffraction patterns of X-rays.

name: DNA Structure
description: Discovery of the double helix structure of deoxyribonucleic acid, the molecular basis of heredity.

name: Molecular Biology
description: The branch of biology that concerns the molecular basis of biological activity between biomolecules in the various systems of a cell.

name: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction)
description: A revolutionary laboratory technique used to rapidly make millions to billions of copies of a specific DNA sample, enabling modern genetic analysis.

name: DNA Sequencing
description: Determining the precise order of nucleotides within a DNA molecule.

name: Recombinant DNA & Genetic Engineering
description: The technology of joining together DNA molecules from different species and inserting it into a host organism to produce new genetic combinations.

name: Bioinformatics
description: Application of computer science and statistics to biological data, especially genomics.

name: Human Genome Project (DNA Mapping)
description: International research effort to determine the sequence of nucleotide base pairs that make up human DNA.

name: Advanced Immunology
description: Deeper understanding of the immune system, leading to advanced vaccines, autoimmune disease treatments, and transplantation.

name: Monoclonal Antibodies
description: Laboratory-produced molecules that serve as substitute antibodies, used for diagnostics and to treat diseases like cancer.

name: Modern Vaccine Development
description: Creation of vaccines (e.g., mRNA, viral vector) using advanced molecular biology and genetic engineering for rapid development and high efficacy.

name: Gene Therapy (Early)
description: Experimental techniques to treat or cure diseases by modifying an individual's genes.

name: Protein Engineering
description: Design and modification of proteins to change binding, catalysis, stability, specificity, or cellular function.

sources.title: Synthetic biology: putting synthesis into biology

name: DNA Synthesis & Oligonucleotides
description: Chemical and enzymatic production of defined DNA sequences used for primers, probes, guide RNAs, genes, and synthetic constructs.

name: RNA Interference
description: Sequence-specific gene silencing by small RNAs, establishing a practical route to programmable knockdown of gene expression.

sources.title: CRISPR-mediated modular RNA-guided regulation of transcription in eukaryotes

name: Zinc-Finger Nucleases
description: Engineered DNA-binding zinc-finger proteins fused to nucleases for programmable genome cleavage before CRISPR became dominant.

name: TALENs
description: Transcription activator-like effector nucleases that use modular protein-DNA recognition to target genome edits.

name: Viral Vectors
description: Engineered viruses used to deliver genetic payloads into cells for research, gene therapy, and genome editing.

sources.title: Drug delivery systems for CRISPR-based genome editors

name: CRISPR Adaptive Immunity
description: The bacterial and archaeal immune system that stores spacer sequences and uses CRISPR-associated proteins to recognize invasive nucleic acids.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity

name: Programmable Cas9 Nuclease
description: Cas9 configured as an RNA-guided nuclease that can be programmed to cut matching DNA sequences.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity

name: Single-Guide RNA Design
description: Design of synthetic guide RNAs that direct Cas enzymes to intended genomic targets while balancing activity and specificity.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity
GUIDE-seq enables genome-wide profiling of off-target cleavage by CRISPR-Cas nucleases

name: PAM Specificity Engineering
description: Selection or engineering of Cas enzymes and targets around protospacer-adjacent motif requirements that constrain editable sites.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity

name: NHEJ and HDR DNA Repair Editing
description: Use of cellular non-homologous end joining and homology-directed repair pathways to convert nuclease cuts into knockouts or precise edits.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity

name: Genetic Engineering
description: Direct manipulation of organism genomes using recombinant DNA, delivery systems, sequencing, and programmable editing tools.

name: CRISPR-Cas9 Genome Editing
description: Programmable genome editing with Cas9 and guide RNAs, enabling targeted cuts, knockouts, repairs, and engineered sequence changes.

sources.title: A programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease in adaptive bacterial immunity

name: CRISPR Off-Target Profiling
description: Genome-wide assays and computational methods for detecting unintended CRISPR nuclease activity and improving editing safety.

sources.title: GUIDE-seq enables genome-wide profiling of off-target cleavage by CRISPR-Cas nucleases

name: Pooled CRISPR Screens
description: Library-scale CRISPR perturbation experiments that link guide RNAs to phenotypes for gene-function discovery.

sources.title: Am I ready for CRISPR? A user’s guide to genetic screens

name: CRISPRi and CRISPRa
description: Catalytically inactive Cas systems fused to regulatory domains to repress or activate genes without cutting DNA.

sources.title: CRISPR-mediated modular RNA-guided regulation of transcription in eukaryotes

name: CRISPR Delivery: AAV, LNP, and RNP
description: Viral vectors, lipid nanoparticles, and ribonucleoprotein delivery formats used to get genome editors into target cells.

sources.title: Drug delivery systems for CRISPR-based genome editors
The 60-year evolution of lipid nanoparticles for nucleic acid delivery

name: Ex Vivo CRISPR Cell Therapy
description: Editing patient or donor cells outside the body, expanding and testing them, then returning them as a therapeutic product.

sources.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapies to Treat Patients with Sickle Cell Disease
Drug delivery systems for CRISPR-based genome editors

name: Base Editing
description: CRISPR-derived editors that chemically convert individual DNA bases without requiring double-strand DNA breaks.

sources.title: Programmable editing of a target base in genomic DNA without double-stranded DNA cleavage
Programmable base editing of A*T to G*C in genomic DNA without DNA cleavage

name: Prime Editing
description: Search-and-replace genome editing that uses a Cas nickase, reverse transcriptase, and prime editing guide RNA to install precise edits.

sources.title: Search-and-replace genome editing without double-strand breaks or donor DNA
Prime editing for precise and highly versatile genome manipulation

name: Cas12 and Cas13 Editing Platforms
description: CRISPR systems beyond Cas9 that expand DNA targeting, RNA targeting, diagnostics, and transcriptome engineering capabilities.

name: Casgevy / Exa-cel
description: The first FDA-approved therapy using CRISPR/Cas9, editing autologous hematopoietic stem cells for sickle cell disease and beta thalassemia.

sources.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapies to Treat Patients with Sickle Cell Disease

name: Systems Biology
description: Computational and mathematical modeling of biological networks, pathways, and phenotypes as interacting systems.

name: Synthetic Biology
description: Engineering biological parts, circuits, cells, and organisms by combining genetic engineering, DNA synthesis, modeling, and standardization.

sources.title: Synthetic biology: applications come of age

name: Stem Cell Research
description: The study of undifferentiated or partially differentiated cells that can differentiate into various cell types, with potential for regenerative medicine.

name: Organ Transplantation
description: Surgical procedures to replace failing organs with healthy ones from donors.

name: X-Ray Imaging
description: Using X-radiation to view the internal structure of an opaque object, revolutionizing medical diagnosis.

name: Computerized Tomography (CT/MRI)
description: Advanced imaging technologies (CT, MRI) that use computer processing to generate cross-sectional images (slices) of the body.

name: Neuroscience
description: Scientific study of the nervous system, including the brain, at molecular, cellular, and cognitive levels.

name: Pharmaceuticals & Drug Design
description: Systematic, often computer-aided, approach to discovering and developing new medications and therapies.

name: Flight (Powered)
description: Achieving sustained, controlled travel through the air with heavier-than-air craft.

name: Jet Engine
description: Airbreathing engine that produces thrust from a fast-moving jet of hot gas.

name: Aerodynamics (Supersonic)
description: Study of air flow at speeds exceeding the speed of sound, crucial for high-performance aircraft and rocketry.

name: Jet Aircraft (High-Speed Flight)
description: Aircraft propelled by jet engines, enabling significantly faster and higher altitude flight.

sources.title: A Brief History of the FAA

name: Supersonic Transport
description: Civilian aircraft designed to fly faster than the speed of sound, such as the Concorde.

name: Helicopters (Vertical Takeoff & Landing - VTOL)
description: Aircraft capable of vertical flight, hovering, and forward/backward movement using rotating blades.

name: Liquid Fuel Propellants
description: Development of liquid chemical propellants for rockets, offering higher performance and control than solid fuels.

name: Rocketry
description: Propulsion technology for reaching high altitudes and space using reaction engines.

sources.title: Sputnik and the Dawn of the Space Age

name: Guidance Systems (Early Inertial)
description: Systems using gyroscopes and accelerometers to navigate vehicles like missiles and spacecraft without external references.

name: Ballistic Missiles (ICBMs)
description: Missiles that follow a ballistic trajectory to deliver one or more warheads to a predetermined target, capable of intercontinental ranges.

name: Space Flight
description: Travel beyond Earth's atmosphere.

sources.title: Sputnik and the Dawn of the Space Age

name: Space Shuttle (Reusable Spacecraft)
description: Development of partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft systems, designed to be launched vertically and land as a glider.

name: Satellite Technology
description: Use of artificial satellites for communication, observation, and navigation.

name: Interplanetary Probes
description: Unmanned robotic spacecraft sent to explore other planets, moons, asteroids, or comets within our solar system.

name: Closed-Loop Life Support (Early Forms)
description: Initial development of systems for recycling air and water in confined environments like submarines and early spacecraft.

name: Space Stations
description: Orbiting artificial structures designed for long-term human habitation and research in space.

name: Radio
description: Wireless transmission of audio signals using electromagnetic waves.

name: Radio Broadcasting
description: Mass distribution of audio content via radio waves to a wide audience.

name: Television
description: System for transmitting visual images and sound that are reproduced on screens, creating a powerful mass medium.

name: Mass Media
description: The collection of media technologies that reach a large audience via mass communication, shaping culture and public opinion.

name: Radar (Radio Detection and Ranging)
description: Using radio waves to determine the range, angle, or velocity of objects.

name: Atomic Clocks
description: Extremely precise timekeeping devices based on atomic resonance.

name: Global Positioning System (GPS)
description: Satellite-based navigation providing location and time data worldwide, requiring relativistic corrections.

name: Lithium-Ion Batteries (Rechargeable Power)
description: High energy density rechargeable batteries widely used in portable electronics and electric vehicles.

name: Mobile Phones
description: Portable communication devices using cellular networks.

name: Mobile Operating Systems (Smartphone OS)
description: Operating systems specifically designed for mobile devices like smartphones and tablets.

name: Smartphones
description: A mobile phone that performs many of the functions of a computer, typically having a touchscreen interface, internet access, and an operating system capable of running downloaded apps.

name: Wi-Fi Wireless Networking
description: Short-range wireless technology enabling devices to connect to local networks and the Internet without cables.

name: Bluetooth
description: Standard for short-range wireless data exchange between devices, often used for peripherals and accessories.

name: Internet of Things (IoT) & Ubiquitous Connectivity
description: Network of physical objects embedded with sensors, software, and other technologies for connecting and exchanging data over the internet.

name: Automobile
description: Self-propelled road vehicle powered by an internal combustion engine, enabling personal mobility on a mass scale.

sources.title: Part I - History - Engineering Data - Interstate System

name: Containerization
description: System of standardized intermodal freight transport using shipping containers, revolutionizing global trade.

name: Nuclear Power
description: Using controlled nuclear fission to produce electricity.

name: Solar Power
description: Harnessing energy from sunlight to produce electricity using photovoltaic cells.

name: Wind Power (Modern)
description: Generating electricity from large-scale wind turbines.

name: Geothermal Power (Earth Heat Energy)
description: Harnessing geothermal energy from the Earth's interior for heating and electricity generation.

sources.title: Grid Modernization and the Smart Grid

name: Renewable Energy Systems
description: Broad adoption and integration of various renewable energy sources (solar, wind, geothermal, hydro) into power grids.

sources.title: Climate Change 2022: Mitigation of Climate Change

name: Plant Breeding & Hybridization
description: Systematic breeding of plants to develop improved varieties with desirable traits like higher yield or disease resistance.

sources.title: Solving Global Climate and Nutrition Challenges Depends on Accelerated Crop Breeding

name: Green Revolution
description: Set of research technology transfer initiatives increasing agricultural production worldwide, particularly with high-yield crop varieties.

sources.title: Solving Global Climate and Nutrition Challenges Depends on Accelerated Crop Breeding

name: Agribusiness
description: The business sector encompassing farming and farming-related commercial activities, involving large-scale production, processing, and distribution.

name: Skyscrapers
description: Very tall buildings made possible by steel frames, reinforced concrete, and elevators.

name: Modern Urban Planning
description: The technical and political process concerned with the development and design of land use and the built environment, including air, water, and infrastructure.

name: Consumerism
description: A social and economic order that encourages the acquisition of goods and services in ever-increasing amounts, driven by mass production and advertising.

name: International Cooperation & Treaties
description: Formal agreements and organizations established between nations to address global issues like trade, war, and the environment.

name: Globalization
description: The process of interaction and integration among people, companies, and governments worldwide, accelerated by technology.

name: Environmentalism & Ecology
description: A scientific, social, and political movement concerned with protecting the natural environment and the systematic study of interactions among organisms and their environment.

sources.title: Climate Change 2022: Mitigation of Climate Change

name: Demography
description: The scientific study of human populations, primarily with respect to their size, structure, and development.

name: Modern Economics (Keynesian)
description: Macroeconomic theories concerning the determinants of total spending in the economy and its effects on output, employment, and inflation.

name: Economics (Behavioral)
description: Study of the effects of psychological, cognitive, emotional, cultural and social factors on the economic decisions of individuals and institutions.

name: Financial Derivatives
description: Complex financial instruments whose value is derived from an underlying asset or group of assets, used for hedging or speculation.

name: Nuclear Weapons
description: Extremely destructive weapons powered by nuclear fission or fusion.

name: Robotics
description: Design and use of autonomous or remote-controlled machines for industrial and other applications.

name: Virtual Reality
description: Immersive computer-simulated environments that can simulate physical presence in real or imagined worlds.

name: Game Consoles (Dedicated Gaming Hardware)
description: Specialized electronic devices for playing video games, connected to a display like a television.

name: Digital Photography (CCD/CMOS Sensors)
description: Capturing images electronically using light-sensitive semiconductor sensors.

name: Hard Disk Drives (Magnetic Storage)
description: Electro-mechanical data storage devices using magnetic storage on rotating platters.

name: Solid State Drives (SSD Flash Memory)
description: Data storage devices using integrated circuit assemblies as memory to store data persistently, typically using flash memory.

name: Digital Signal Processing (DSP)
description: Manipulation of signals (audio, video, etc.) that have been converted into digital form.

name: Cell Culture
description: Growing cells outside living organisms in controlled media, enabling virology, vaccines, monoclonal antibodies, toxicology, and regenerative medicine.

name: Electron Microscope
description: Microscopy that uses electron beams rather than visible light to image structures at nanometer scales.

name: Advanced Electron Microscopy
description: High-resolution scanning and transmission electron microscopy with digital detectors, sample preparation, and computational reconstruction.

name: Space-Based Advanced Optics
description: Precision telescopes, spectrometers, interferometers, and cooled detectors operating beyond atmospheric distortion.

name: Advanced Meteorology
description: Weather science based on remote sensing, radiosondes, satellites, numerical models, radar, and global observation networks.

sources.title: Satellite Data
sources.publisher: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information

name: Computational Linguistics
description: Computational modeling of language structure, meaning, speech, and text for translation, search, dialogue, and language understanding.

name: Deep Drilling Technology
description: High-pressure drilling systems, downhole sensors, mud engineering, and directional techniques for accessing deep geothermal and hydrocarbon resources.

name: Graphics Processing Units (GPUs)
description: Massively parallel processors optimized for graphics and numerical workloads, later becoming critical for simulation and machine learning.

name: Deep Learning Neural Networks
description: Training large multi-layer neural networks on abundant data and accelerated hardware for vision, speech, language, and prediction tasks.

sources.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks

name: Transformer Architectures
description: Attention-based neural network architectures that scale efficiently across text, code, images, audio, and multimodal data.

name: Large Language Models
description: Very large transformer-based models trained on broad corpora to generate, summarize, translate, reason over, and transform language and code.

name: Diffusion Models & Generative AI
description: Generative models that learn to synthesize images, audio, video, molecules, and other data by reversing a gradual noising process.

name: mRNA Vaccines
description: Vaccines that deliver messenger RNA instructions for cells to produce antigens and stimulate targeted immune protection.

name: Lipid Nanoparticles
description: Nanoscale lipid carriers used to protect and deliver fragile therapeutic molecules such as RNA into cells.

name: Container Orchestration
description: Automated deployment, scheduling, scaling, and recovery of containerized software services across clusters of machines.

name: Edge Computing
description: Moving computation and storage closer to users, sensors, and devices to reduce latency, bandwidth use, and central infrastructure dependence.

name: Zero Trust Security
description: Security architecture that continuously verifies identity, device state, policy, and context instead of trusting a network perimeter.

name: Packet Switching
description: Breaking digital messages into addressed packets routed independently across shared networks, the foundation of scalable data networking.

name: Public Key Infrastructure
description: Certificate authorities, digital certificates, and trust chains for authenticating identities and securing network communication.

name: Content Delivery Networks
description: Distributed caches and routing systems that serve web content closer to users for lower latency and higher resilience.

name: Open Source Software
description: Collaborative software development with publicly available source code, permissive or copyleft licenses, and distributed maintenance communities.

name: Relational Query Languages (SQL)
description: Declarative languages for defining, querying, and updating relational databases at enterprise scale.

name: Recommender Systems
description: Algorithms that rank and personalize products, media, search results, and social feeds from user behavior and item data.

name: Deep Learning Computer Vision
description: Neural network systems that classify, detect, segment, and generate visual content using large datasets and accelerated training.

sources.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks

name: Deep Learning Speech Recognition
description: Neural transcription and speech understanding systems used in dictation, assistants, captioning, call centers, and accessibility tools.

name: Messenger RNA Therapeutics
description: Programmable RNA medicines for vaccines, protein replacement, cancer immunotherapy, and rapid-response therapeutics.

name: Grid-Scale Battery Storage
description: Large battery installations that balance electrical grids, integrate variable renewable power, and provide backup capacity.

name: Precision Agriculture
description: Sensor, satellite, GPS, data, and automation techniques for optimizing irrigation, fertilizer, seeding, and harvest decisions field by field.

sources.title: High-Tech Agriculture Continues to Reap Rewards for Farmers and Society

name: Digital Twins
description: Computational replicas of machines, factories, cities, or bodies used for monitoring, simulation, prediction, and control.

name: Supercomputing
description: High-performance computing systems using parallel processors, fast interconnects, and specialized software to solve large scientific and engineering problems.

name: MEMS Sensors
description: Microelectromechanical accelerometers, gyroscopes, pressure sensors, and microphones fabricated with semiconductor-like processes.

name: Flash Memory
description: Non-volatile semiconductor memory that can be electrically erased and rewritten for cards, phones, SSDs, and embedded devices.

name: Touchscreen Interfaces
description: Direct-manipulation displays using resistive, capacitive, optical, or acoustic sensing for phones, kiosks, tablets, and control panels.

name: Satellite Remote Sensing
description: Orbital imaging and spectral measurement of land, oceans, atmosphere, crops, cities, and disasters for science and operations.

name: Earth System Models
description: Coupled computational models of atmosphere, oceans, ice, land, biosphere, and human emissions for climate and environmental forecasting.

name: Continuous Integration
description: Automated builds, tests, and checks that integrate software changes frequently and catch regressions early.

name: Microfluidics
description: Precise manipulation of tiny liquid volumes in channels and chips for diagnostics, chemistry, biology, and lab automation.

name: Operations Research
description: Mathematical optimization, simulation, queuing, and decision analysis for logistics, warfare, industry, finance, and public planning.

name: Systems Engineering
description: Interdisciplinary methods for designing, integrating, testing, and managing complex technical systems across their life cycle.

name: Weather Satellites
description: Orbital platforms for observing clouds, storms, sea surface temperature, atmospheric water vapor, and climate variables.

name: Solar Photovoltaics
description: Semiconductor devices that convert sunlight directly into electricity for calculators, satellites, rooftops, and utility-scale power.

name: Wind Turbine Farms
description: Arrays of aerodynamic turbines, power electronics, controls, and grid connections for large-scale wind electricity generation.

name: API Economy
description: Business and software ecosystems built around programmatic interfaces for payments, maps, identity, logistics, AI, and data services.

name: Software Supply Chain Security
description: Practices for securing dependencies, build systems, provenance, code signing, package registries, and deployment pipelines.

name: Vector Databases
description: Data systems optimized for storing embeddings and retrieving semantically similar text, images, audio, code, and other high-dimensional data.

sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: AI architecture that combines generative models with external search or databases to ground responses in retrieved information.

sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks

name: Laser Scanners
description: Optical systems that use laser light and detectors to read codes, measure surfaces, scan documents, and map environments.

name: Barcodes
description: Machine-readable optical codes for tracking products, inventory, shipments, tickets, documents, and manufacturing flows.

name: RFID Tracking
description: Radio-frequency identification tags and readers for contactless inventory, logistics, access, payments, and asset tracking.

name: Reverse Osmosis Desalination
description: Membrane-based removal of salts and impurities from seawater or brackish water for municipal, industrial, and emergency supply.

name: Protein Sequencing
description: Methods for determining amino acid sequences, linking genes, enzymes, structure, and biological function.

name: Single-Cell Sequencing
description: Sequencing methods that measure genetic or transcriptomic information from individual cells to reveal hidden biological variation.

name: Lab-on-a-Chip
description: Miniaturized devices that integrate sample handling, reactions, separation, sensing, and analysis on small fluidic chips.

name: Federated Learning
description: Machine learning across distributed devices or organizations while keeping raw data local for privacy, latency, or governance reasons.

name: Digital Identity Systems
description: Technical and institutional systems for authenticating people, organizations, devices, and services online.

sources.title: NIST Special Publication 800-63 Digital Identity Guidelines

name: Green Hydrogen

description: Fabrication method using silicon dioxide masking and surface passivation to make reliable, mass-producible silicon devices.

name: MOSFET Transistors
description: Field-effect transistors controlled through an insulated gate, becoming the dominant switching device for dense digital integrated circuits.

name: CMOS Logic
description: Complementary MOS circuits using paired n-channel and p-channel MOSFETs for low-power digital logic.

name: Photoresist Chemistry
description: Light-sensitive polymer systems that define fine circuit patterns during semiconductor lithography.

name: Semiconductor Photomasks
description: High-precision patterned masks or reticles used to project circuit layouts onto photoresist-coated wafers.

name: Ion Implantation
description: Semiconductor doping method that accelerates ions into wafers to precisely control transistor electrical properties.

name: Plasma Etching
description: Dry etching process that uses reactive plasmas to remove selected thin-film materials in microfabrication.

name: Chemical Vapor Deposition
description: Thin-film deposition process that forms solid materials on wafers from reactive gases.

name: Atomic Layer Deposition
description: Self-limiting thin-film deposition technique that builds materials one atomic-scale layer at a time.

name: Chemical Mechanical Planarization
description: Wafer polishing process combining slurry chemistry and mechanical abrasion to flatten multilayer integrated circuits.

name: DUV Stepper Lithography
description: Deep-ultraviolet projection lithography using steppers and scanners to pattern advanced semiconductor layers.

name: EUV Lithography
description: Extreme-ultraviolet lithography using 13.5 nm light to print the smallest high-volume chip features.

name: Electronic Design Automation
description: Software tools for specifying, simulating, verifying, placing, routing, and checking integrated-circuit designs.

name: Hardware Description Languages
description: Languages such as Verilog and VHDL for describing, simulating, and synthesizing digital hardware.

name: VLSI Design
description: Methodology for designing very-large-scale integrated circuits with thousands to billions of transistors.

name: RISC Microprocessors
description: Processor architectures using simplified instruction sets, pipelining, and compiler support for high-performance chips.

description: Integrated circuit that combines processor cores, memory controllers, interfaces, accelerators, and peripherals on one die.

name: DRAM Memory
description: Dynamic random-access memory storing bits as charge in capacitor cells refreshed by control circuitry.

description: Static memory built from transistor latches for fast on-chip caches and buffers.

name: Semiconductor Process Nodes
description: Generations of semiconductor manufacturing defined by device architecture, density, lithography, interconnect, and process design rules.

description: Three-dimensional field-effect transistors with fin-shaped channels that improve electrostatic control at small process nodes.

sources.title: Accelerating AI and HPC with Advanced Technologies

name: Gate-All-Around Nanosheet Transistors
description: Transistors whose gates wrap around stacked nanosheet channels for continued logic scaling beyond FinFETs.

sources.title: Accelerating AI and HPC with Advanced Technologies

roadmap.rationale: Gate-all-around devices improve channel control after FinFET scaling limits and are already tied to announced advanced process nodes.

name: Backside Power Delivery
description: Chip fabrication approach routing power through the back of the wafer to reduce front-side interconnect congestion and voltage droop.

sources.title: Accelerating AI and HPC with Advanced Technologies

roadmap.rationale: Moving power wiring to the wafer backside can free front-side routing resources and reduce voltage losses in dense logic.

name: High-NA EUV Lithography
description: Next-generation EUV lithography with higher numerical-aperture optics for smaller features and reduced multipatterning.

sources.title: Accelerating AI and HPC with Advanced Technologies

roadmap.rationale: High-NA EUV is the clearest near-term extension of optical lithography for leading-edge patterning when low-NA EUV multipatterning becomes too costly.

name: Advanced Semiconductor Packaging 2.5D/3D
description: Integration of multiple dies with interposers, fan-out, wafer bonding, or vertical stacking to improve bandwidth, density, and yield.

name: Through-Silicon Vias
description: Vertical electrical connections etched through silicon dies or interposers for dense 3D and 2.5D chip integration.

name: High Bandwidth Memory
description: Stacked DRAM memory using through-silicon vias and very wide interfaces for high-throughput GPUs and accelerators.

name: Chiplet Architecture
description: Processor design approach that composes multiple smaller dies into one package to improve yield, scaling, and product reuse.

sources.title: National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Formula Program Fact Sheet

name: High-Speed Rail
description: Rail systems with dedicated track, electric traction, signaling, and trainsets designed for sustained high-speed passenger service.

name: Powder Metallurgy
description: Manufacturing methods that compact and sinter metal powders to make precise parts, hard materials, filters, and specialized alloys.

name: Advanced Ceramics
description: Engineered ceramic materials with high hardness, heat resistance, insulation, biocompatibility, or electronic properties.

name: Nickel Superalloys
description: High-temperature alloys engineered for strength, creep resistance, corrosion resistance, and turbine performance.

name: High-Performance Polymers
description: Engineered polymers with exceptional thermal, chemical, mechanical, or electrical performance for aerospace, electronics, medical, and industrial use.

sources.title: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 1963 - Speed Read

name: Shape-Memory Alloys
description: Alloys that recover preset shapes under temperature or stress changes, enabling actuators, medical devices, couplings, and adaptive systems.

name: Graphene & Two-Dimensional Materials
description: Atomically thin materials such as graphene with unusual electrical, mechanical, thermal, and optical properties.

name: Materials Informatics
description: Data-driven materials discovery using databases, simulations, machine learning, and high-throughput experiments.

name: Advanced Materials Science
description: Computational, experimental, and nanoscale methods for designing and discovering materials with targeted mechanical, electronic, optical, thermal, or chemical properties.
